sourceRef.citation: د. ابوبكر عبدالله الحبتي et al. 2027

statement.en: Influence of the Arctic Oscillation on the variability of winter mean temperatures in Libya
statement.summary: The Arctic Oscillation (AO) effects the climate of the northern hemisphere. Several previous studies have been shown the effect of the Arctic Oscillation (AO) on climate conditions in winter in America, Europe, Asia and the Mediterranean region; Therefore, this study attempts to investigate the effect of the Arctic Oscillation (AO) on the mean temperature, As well as the mean maximum and minimum winter temperatures in Libya (December-January-February) by analyzing the correlation for 11 stations over 50 years (1961-2010) and testing it statistically. It was found that the results of the temperature relationship of the winter months are negatively related between the Arctic Oscillation (AO) and all the mean temperatures for the winter months in all the study stations, The study also reveale

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: The axiom presents an empirical climate study (1961-2010 dataset, 11 Libyan stations) with stated findings of negative AO-temperature correlation. However: (1) Abstract is incomplete/truncated ('The study also reveale'), suggesting unvalidated/preprint status; (2) Metadata inconsistency: publication date 2026-04-25 is future-dated relative to any current evaluation; (3) Categorized as cs.AI despite being atmospheric science/climatology, indicating possible miscategorization or meta-study status; (4) Core claim (AO influence on Libyan winter temps) is empirically testable but pending full peer review and complete manuscript validation.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 北極の息吹が遠く砂漠を冷やす—距離を無視した共鳴。
